overview_purpose_1.description: Explains marimo's purpose as a reactive Python notebook, why it emphasizes reproducibility and app/script reuse, and how its public library supports interactive notebooks.

setup_cli_1.description: Explains how marimo is installed, how the command-line interface introduces editing, running, tutorials, conversion, export, and how the main Click commands route those workflows.

public_interface_1.description: Explains how marimo exposes its top-level Python API, how the API docs group the interface, and when developers should use key public helpers for apps, markdown, state, outputs, and console capture.

reactive_runtime_dataflow_1.description: Explains how marimo derives a notebook dependency graph from cell definitions and references, how that graph drives reactive execution, stale and disabled behavior, and dataflow visualization.

apps_scripts_wasm_1.description: Explains how marimo notebooks are run as web apps, ordinary Python scripts, exported artifacts, and browser-only WebAssembly notebooks.

ui_outputs_interactivity_1.description: Explains how marimo connects interactive UI elements, cell reactivity, markdown and rich outputs, and imperative output updates for app-like notebooks.

sql_data_integrations_1.description: Explains how marimo SQL cells execute queries, return dataframe-like results, discover database connections and schemas, and connect notebook data workflows to external data sources.

ai_mcp_pairing_1.description: Explains how marimo connects its notebook-aware AI assistant, MCP server and client support, external agent pairing, and shared tool registration model.
